prompt: Single-frame, physically plausible, cinematic editorial photograph. Interior of an old locksmith workshop at dusk, one workbench in sharp focus. On the bench: a highly intricate brass key with excessive tiny teeth and ridges, handcrafted to match one unusually damaged antique lock full of scratches, dents, and asymmetrical wear. The key is currently turning perfectly in that one damaged lock. Around them, arranged naturally on the same bench, are 12 clean, standard locks of the same family; each has fresh scratch marks around its keyway where the intricate key clearly failed to enter or turn. No text, no numbers, no labels, no diagrams, no equations, no symbols, no HUD overlays. The scene must feel real and photographable, with authentic metal textures, machining marks, fingerprints, and wood dust. Visual logic should immediately convey: perfect fit to idiosyncratic noise in one example, poor generalization to the broader population. Composition: rule-of-thirds, shallow depth of field centered on the single matched lock and key, failed locks visible in midground and background. Lighting: warm tungsten on the matched pair, cooler ambient light on the failed locks to emphasize contrast. High detail, natural optics, magazine-feature quality. Negative prompt: no chart, no graph, no floating interface, no neon data streams, no glowing neural brain, no gears metaphor, no text watermark, no collage, no split panel, no impossible physics.
metaphor: An over-engineered key opens one damaged lock perfectly but fails on many normal locks, mirroring memorization of noise and weak generalization.
